---
title: "Summary of findings (GRADE — draft)"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(readr); library(knitr)
})
gen <- "_generated"
grade  <- read_csv(file.path(gen, "grade_draft.csv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)
pooled <- read_csv(file.path(gen, "pooled_summary.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
sof <- pooled |>
  inner_join(grade |> select(outcome, certainty), by = "outcome") |>
  transmute(
    Outcome = stringr::str_to_title(outcome),
    `k (studies)` = k,
    `Pooled OR (95% CI)` = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f – %.2f)", pooled_OR, ci_lo, ci_hi),
    `I²` = sprintf("%.0f%%", I2),
    Certainty = certainty)
kable(sof)
```

The certainty column is a *mechanical draft* derived from the rules pre-specified
in `protocol/PRISMA-P_protocol.md` §15c and the GRADE handbook (Schünemann et
al., 2013). The review team finalises each judgement after explicit discussion
of indirectness — which the rule engine does not assess automatically — and
any plausible-confounding upgrade considerations.

A full domain-by-domain rationale is recorded in `data/grade.csv` once
finalised.
